proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between three leading panel identification estimators that combine pre-treatment unit weighting with time-period reweighting: the original Synthetic DiD (Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, Wager 2021, AER) with double-difference weights chosen by quadratic regularization, the Augmented Synthetic Control / matrix completion estimator (Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, Khosravi 2021, JASA) with nuclear-norm penalization on the donor-pool outcome matrix, and the staggered Synthetic DiD with cohort-time weights (Arkhangelsky-Imbens-Lei-Luo 2024, Quantitative Economics) extending SDiD to multiple treated cohorts. All three target the average treatment effect on the treated (ATT) under interactive fixed effects with bounded factor-loading rank R. The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published synthetic-control / matrix completion estimators OR axis c: strict extension to bounded-rank interactive-FE regime): characterize the sharp boundary in (R, T_pre, N_donor) parameter space at which the three estimators have asymptotically equivalent identifying functionals versus strictly different limit functionals. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: SDiD, ASCM, and staggered-SDiD identify the same ATT and achieve identical asymptotic variance if and only if R <= R_star where R_star is a sharp threshold function of the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel matrix combining the donor pre-period outcome covariance, the cohort-time treated outcome regression, and the regularization strength sequence; when R > R_star, the three estimators have strictly different limit functionals — SDiD has bias proportional to the spectral gap, ASCM is consistent but inefficient by a factor depending on the nuclear-norm strength, and staggered-SDiD achieves the semiparametric efficiency bound. R_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments + regularization sequence, NOT an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the well-known equivalence under low-rank interactive FE (R=0 or R<<T_pre) and provides the first sharp triple-non-equivalence boundary for SDiD / matrix completion / staggered-SDiD in closed form.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-repo prior art is pid_synth_control_factor_misspec, which studied partial-identification intervals under factor misspecification and left row-span visibility as an assumption; this proposal instead asks for exact-identification equality and strict separation among convex SCM, signed/ridge ASCM, SDiD, and rank-R MC functionals. The failed eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1 spectral-threshold proposal is adjacent only in its reviewer demand for primitive matrix thresholds; it targeted event-study efficiency rather than the SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC span lattice.","published_axis":"Closest published results are DoudchenkoImbens2016, BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021, ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021, and AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021. The epsilon-gap is that they define or motivate individual estimator classes and consistency routes, but do not give observable necessary-and-sufficient equality certificates or open-set strict separation witnesses across the full convex SCM -> signed/ridge ASCM -> SDiD -> rank-R MC lattice."}
seed_list: Prior work ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 establishes SDiD consistency under IFE conditions; our seed conjectures that equality with ASCM and MC holds only on the null space of an observable spectral mismatch kernel, because time weighting and donor augmentation impose different row-span projections.

Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 would imply that low-rank MC and synthetic-control routes solve the same causal-panel prediction problem; our seed conjectures that nuclear-norm shrinkage and SDiD quadratic balancing separate whenever the post-period Riesz representer escapes the donor pre-period covariance kernel.

Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 establishes ridge ASCM as debiasing imperfect SCM fit; our seed conjectures that the ridge correction equals SDiD time weighting only when a signed-weight extrapolation direction is annihilated by a Schur complement, because augmentation and time reweighting use dual but nonidentical moments.

Prior work DoudchenkoImbens2016 synthesizes SCM, DiD, and regression through weight restrictions; our seed conjectures a strict lattice in which convex SCM, affine/ridge ASCM, SDiD, and MC are inclusions with minimal rank-R witnesses for every strict separation.

Prior work AbadieLHour2021 establishes that penalized SCM changes donor-weight geometry; our seed conjectures that penalty scaling creates phase transitions in the ATT limit functional, because fixed, vanishing, and exploding penalties select different faces of the donor pre-period span.

Prior work ArkhangelskyImbensLeiLuo2024 establishes design-robust panel weights for general treatment paths; our seed conjectures that staggered SDiD collapses to a cohort-weighted sum of single-shock SDiD only when later-treated controls generate no off-diagonal factor block.

Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2022 establishes partially pooled SCM for staggered adoption; our seed conjectures that partial pooling and cohort-time SDiD separate on open rank-R panels whenever average treated imbalance and unit-specific imbalance have different singular directions.

Prior work BaiNg2021 establishes factor-imputation normal theory for ATT; our seed conjectures that factor-imputation and SDiD have incomparable asymptotic variance under one bounded-rank nuisance tangent space because their influence functions project onto different observable kernels.

Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 establishes efficient imputation estimators in additive staggered DiD; our seed conjectures that the BJS efficiency ordering fails under bounded-rank IFE unless the factor tangent space is orthogonal to the SDiD/MC mismatch kernel.

Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 notes causal-panel methods differ in identification restrictions; our seed conjectures that the exact equality set is computable from observable second moments plus penalty sequences, because all three normal equations share a finite Schur-complement obstruction.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 establishes SDiD consistency under IFE conditions; our seed conjectures that equality with ASCM and MC holds only on the null space of an observable spectral mismatch kernel, because time weighting and donor augmentation impose different row-span projections.

anchor_paper.author: Dmitry Arkhangelsky, Susan Athey, David A. Hirshberg, Guido W. Imbens, Stefan Wager
anchor_paper.claim: SDiD combines unit weights and time weights and proves IFE consistency/asymptotic normality, but not a sharp finite-dimensional equivalence characterization against ASCM or matrix completion.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed is not T=2-to-general-T; it predicts a sign/equality phase boundary where the SDiD functional ceases to equal named ASCM/MC functionals.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 because their IFE consistency already implies equality under low rank.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: consistency of SDiD does not identify the necessary-and-sufficient equality set against ridge ASCM and nuclear-norm MC under shared observable moments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 would imply that low-rank MC and synthetic-control routes solve the same causal-panel prediction problem; our seed conjectures that nuclear-norm shrinkage and SDiD quadratic balancing separate whenever the post-period Riesz representer escapes the donor pre-period covariance kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Susan Athey, Mohsen Bayati, Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens, Khashayar Khosravi
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Matrix completion connects low-rank regularization, IFE, synthetic control, and unconfoundedness, but does not give necessary-and-sufficient equality conditions with SDiD or ASCM.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/equivalence-boundary pattern, not blocked: it targets the named MC estimator and promotes separation to an open class via a kernel condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 because they show all estimators focus on the same objective function.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that paper emphasizes related objectives but says methods differ in identification restrictions; it does not give the equality zero set or strict separation frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 establishes ridge ASCM as debiasing imperfect SCM fit; our seed conjectures that the ridge correction equals SDiD time weighting only when a signed-weight extrapolation direction is annihilated by a Schur complement, because augmentation and time reweighting use dual but nonidentical moments.

anchor_paper.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: ASCM debiases imperfect synthetic-control fit using an outcome model and ridge negative weights, but leaves the exact equality frontier with SDiD and MC unstated.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens-assumption pattern avoided: the mechanism is a dual-moment annihilation condition causing equality/separation, not broader assumptions for ASCM.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 because ridge ASCM already allows negative weights and controls pre-fit.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: ASCM's ridge representation does not compare its limiting ATT functional to SDiD time weights or nuclear-norm MC.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DoudchenkoImbens2016 synthesizes SCM, DiD, and regression through weight restrictions; our seed conjectures a strict lattice in which convex SCM, affine/ridge ASCM, SDiD, and MC are inclusions with minimal rank-R witnesses for every strict separation.

anchor_paper.author: Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: The synthesis paper relaxes SCM restrictions through intercepts, negative weights, non-unit-sum weights, and regularization, but does not prove a formal strict hierarchy among later SDiD, ASCM, and MC estimands.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Strict-extension pattern, not blocked: the extension is required to produce strict minimal rank-R witnesses for named published estimators, not just a broader class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DoudchenkoImbens2016 because relaxing restrictions gives an obvious hierarchy.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: once SDiD time weights and MC shrinkage enter, feasible identifying functionals are not obviously nested and need witnesses.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AbadieLHour2021 establishes that penalized SCM changes donor-weight geometry; our seed conjectures that penalty scaling creates phase transitions in the ATT limit functional, because fixed, vanishing, and exploding penalties select different faces of the donor pre-period span.

anchor_paper.author: Alberto Abadie, Jeremy L'Hour
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Penalized SCM shows different regularizations select different synthetic controls in disaggregated settings, exposing non-equivalent feasible-weight geometries.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Regularization phase-transition pattern, not blocked: the claim is a change in population ATT functional across penalty regimes, not applying the same penalty elsewhere.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AbadieLHour2021 because penalized SCM already studies penalty choice.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Abadie-L'Hour concerns penalized SCM geometry, not a multi-estimator ATT limit phase diagram involving SDiD, ASCM, and MC.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ArkhangelskyImbensLeiLuo2024 establishes design-robust panel weights for general treatment paths; our seed conjectures that staggered SDiD collapses to a cohort-weighted sum of single-shock SDiD only when later-treated controls generate no off-diagonal factor block.

anchor_paper.author: Dmitry Arkhangelsky, Guido W. Imbens, Lihua Lei, Xiaoman Luo
anchor_paper.claim: Design-robust TWFE constructs assignment-model weights for general treatment paths including staggered adoption and links weighting functions to limiting estimands, but does not give a sharp equivalence theorem to SDiD or ASCM under IFE.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends Bernoulli/single-shock to staggered pattern, not blocked: the mechanism is an off-diagonal factor block that creates a strictly different functional.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ArkhangelskyImbensLeiLuo2024 because their weighting functions characterize limiting estimands for treatment paths.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their design-weight characterization does not provide a rank-IFE equivalence condition to single-shock SDiD or ASCM.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BenMichaelFellerRothstein2022 establishes partially pooled SCM for staggered adoption; our seed conjectures that partial pooling and cohort-time SDiD separate on open rank-R panels whenever average treated imbalance and unit-specific imbalance have different singular directions.

anchor_paper.author: Eli Ben-Michael, Avi Feller, Jesse Rothstein
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B
anchor_paper.claim: Partially pooled SCM for staggered adoption balances both unit-specific and average treated-unit imbalance, showing that single-component balancing can bias the average effect.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation pattern, not blocked: the named phenomenon is singular-direction conflict between two published balancing objects.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BenMichaelFellerRothstein2022 because they already show single-component balancing can bias average effects.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: they do not compare partially pooled SCM to cohort-time SDiD under a bounded-rank spectral separation condition.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BaiNg2021 establishes factor-imputation normal theory for ATT; our seed conjectures that factor-imputation and SDiD have incomparable asymptotic variance under one bounded-rank nuisance tangent space because their influence functions project onto different observable kernels.

anchor_paper.author: Jushan Bai, Serena Ng
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Factor-imputation matrix completion establishes normal distribution theory for ATT estimators under strong factor structure, on a different route from SDiD weighting.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Efficiency-ordering pattern, not blocked: it predicts non-dominance under a shared tangent space, not a routine variance calculation for one estimator.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BaiNg2021 because they derive normal theory for factor-imputation ATT.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: normal theory for one estimator does not give pairwise influence-function ordering against SDiD/ASCM/MC under a common nuisance class.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 establishes efficient imputation estimators in additive staggered DiD; our seed conjectures that the BJS efficiency ordering fails under bounded-rank IFE unless the factor tangent space is orthogonal to the SDiD/MC mismatch kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Xavier Jaravel, Jann Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: For staggered DiD/event studies the paper derives robust and efficient estimators, but in an additive parallel-trends framework rather than the SDiD/ASCM/MC bounded-rank IFE geometry.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample pattern, not blocked: it targets a named efficiency result and proposes a nondegenerate bounded-rank IFE obstruction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 because their efficiency theory is already for staggered panels.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their framework is additive parallel trends, not bounded-rank IFE with SDiD/MC spectral mismatch.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 notes causal-panel methods differ in identification restrictions; our seed conjectures that the exact equality set is computable from observable second moments plus penalty sequences, because all three normal equations share a finite Schur-complement obstruction.

anchor_paper.author: Susan Athey, Mohsen Bayati, Nikolay Doudchenko, Guido W. Imbens, Khashayar Khosravi
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: MC identifies through low nuclear-norm regularization and notes that causal-panel approaches differ in how they impose identification restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; it is an equality-set characterization for named objectives rather than an assumption weakening or target relabeling.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the first-order conditions for ridge and nuclear-norm objectives because all are projections.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the shared Schur-complement obstruction across SDiD time weights, ASCM ridge augmentation, and MC shrinkage is not derived in the literature and includes sharp strict-separation witnesses.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 supplied the literature map and it subsumes legacy Step 0a: Abadie and Gardeazabal (2003) plus Abadie, Diamond, and Hainmueller (2010, 2015) establish convex SCM foundations; Abadie (2021 JEL) codifies feasibility and reporting diagnostics. Doudchenko and Imbens (2016) synthesize SCM, regression, and DiD as restrictions on intercepts, weights, and regularization. Xu (2017) and Gobillon-Magnac (2016) place SCM inside interactive fixed-effects rank geometry. Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi (2021 JASA) develop nuclear-norm matrix completion for causal panels and connect MC, IFE, SCM, and unconfoundedness. Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, and Wager (2021 AER) introduce SDiD with unit and time weights and IFE asymptotics. Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2021 JASA) introduce ridge ASCM and controlled negative-weight extrapolation. Bai and Ng (2021 JASA) give factor-imputation normal theory for ATT. Ferman and Pinto (2021 QE) show imperfect pre-treatment fit can bias SCM-type estimators. Abadie and L'Hour (2021 JASA) show penalization changes SCM feasible-weight geometry. Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2022 JRSS-B) and Arkhangelsky, Imbens, Lei, and Luo (2024 QE) extend synthetic/control-weight ideas toward staggered and design-robust panels. The live frontier is a sharp equivalence/separation calculus in rank R, pre-period count, donor count, adoption support, and penalty scaling.
